widgets_values: Describe the full story and visual style, then run. Reddit-improved v2: Gemini now writes a continuity-focused character bible into panel prompts, uses fixed 4-panel pacing (establishing, action, reaction/clue, payoff), checks previous frames as visual references, and outputs a fixed 2x2 comic grid with white gutters.
Story about Ronaldo eating ramen; the ramen suddenly disappears; Ronaldo searches for it and discovers that a cat ate all his ramen; anime comic style

nodes.title: Prompt (What is story about)
widgets_values: Story about Ronaldo eating ramen; the ramen suddenly disappears; Ronaldo searches for it and discovers that a cat ate all his ramen; anime comic style
Story about Ronaldo eating ramen; the ramen suddenly disappears; Ronaldo searches for it and discovers that a cat ate all his ramen; anime comic style

You are the continuity director for a four-panel comic page. Convert the user's short story into one ready-to-use image prompt for PANEL 1 ONLY.

Output only the final image prompt. No bullets, no explanation, no JSON.

Panel 1 role: establishing shot. Introduce the main character, setting, outfit, key prop, mood, and the first story beat.
Continuity requirement: write exact reusable character details directly into the prompt: face shape, hairstyle, hair color, age range, outfit colors, signature prop, and art style. These details must be easy to repeat in later panels.
Composition: one square comic panel only, not a full page layout. Use a clear foreground/midground/background, safe margins, readable silhouette, and a cinematic wide or medium-wide camera angle.
Style: follow the user's requested style exactly; keep the palette, linework, and rendering style consistent for all panels.
Text: include at most one short readable speech bubble or caption only if it improves the story; otherwise specify no text.
End the prompt with: same character design and outfit in all panels, consistent identity, panel 1 of 4.

You are the continuity director for a four-panel comic page. Use the attached previous panel as the visual reference and the text context to create one ready-to-use image prompt for PANEL 2 ONLY.

Output only the final image prompt. No bullets, no explanation, no JSON.

Panel 2 role: action or complication. Advance the story clearly from panel 1 and change the camera angle from panel 1, preferably medium shot, over-the-shoulder, or dynamic action framing.
Continuity requirement: keep the same main character identity, face, hairstyle, outfit colors, prop details, art style, lighting, and palette as the reference image. Do not change age, gender presentation, costume, hair color, or facial structure.
Composition: one square comic panel only, not a full page layout. Leave safe margins and room for one small speech bubble/caption if needed.
Text: include at most one short readable bubble/caption if it helps; no random text.
End the prompt with: same character as the reference image, same outfit and art style, panel 2 of 4.
Story about Ronaldo eating ramen; the ramen suddenly disappears; Ronaldo searches for it and discovers that a cat ate all his ramen; anime comic style

You are the continuity director for a four-panel comic page. Use the attached reference board of previous panels and the text context to create one ready-to-use image prompt for PANEL 3 ONLY.

Output only the final image prompt. No bullets, no explanation, no JSON.

Panel 3 role: reaction, clue, or emotional turn. Make the pacing feel comic-like by using a different shot size from panels 1 and 2, preferably close-up, insert shot, reaction shot, or low-angle reveal.
Continuity requirement: preserve the exact same main character identity, face, hairstyle, outfit, prop details, linework, palette, and setting logic from the reference board. Repeat important character descriptors directly in the prompt.
Composition: one square comic panel only, not a page or collage. Use strong readable expression and clear story action.
Text: include at most one short readable bubble/caption if it helps; no random text.
End the prompt with: same character as previous panels, same outfit and art style, panel 3 of 4.

You are the continuity director for a four-panel comic page. Use the attached reference board of previous panels and the text context to create one ready-to-use image prompt for PANEL 4 ONLY.

Output only the final image prompt. No bullets, no explanation, no JSON.

Panel 4 role: payoff or resolution. Make the ending readable at a glance and vary the camera from panel 3, preferably wide reveal, dramatic final beat, or comedic punchline composition.
Continuity requirement: preserve the same main character identity, face, hairstyle, outfit, prop details, art style, lighting, and palette from all previous panels. Repeat the core character descriptors directly in the prompt.
Composition: one square comic panel only, not a page or collage. Clear final action, safe margins, no cropped faces, no extra unrelated characters.
Text: include at most one short readable bubble/caption if it delivers the punchline; no random text.
End the prompt with: same character as previous panels, same outfit and art style, panel 4 of 4.
